mission.title: envoy: Client side ALPN support (upstream cluster)
mission.description: Client side ALPN support (upstream cluster) . Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current envoy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your envoy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
helm list -n envoy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working envoy installation.

steps.title: Review envoy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant envoy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get configmap -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=envoy
```
*Title*: *Envoy should support client side protocol negotiation via alpn*

*Description*:
At present if the an upstream cluster has `http2_protocolOptions`, it will always send h2 traffic upstream.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Client side ALPN support (upstream cluster)
steps.description:
Optimally, this would be done as part of also solving https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/1502. The right solution here is to have a new connection pool implementation that can do both HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, ALPN negotiation, upgrade, etc. I would prefer to not complicate the existing connection pool implementations with this logic.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Upgrade envoy to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade envoy:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade envoy envoy/envoy --namespace envoy
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy
helm list -n envoy
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get events -n envoy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Client side ALPN support (upstream cluster) " is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Optimally, this would be done as part of also solving https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/1502. The right solution here is to have a new connection pool implementation that can do both HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, ALPN negotiation, upgrade, etc. I would prefer to not complicate the existing connection pool implementations with this logic.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with envoy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
